title: Sarepta Therapeutics Q1 2026 Earnings Beat Overshadowed by ELEVIDYS Revenue Dip
text:
Sarepta Therapeutics reported Q1 2026 results that beat analyst expectations on earnings but revealed concerning softness in the flagship ELEVIDYS product. The company reported total revenues of $730.8 million and net product revenues of $330.5 million, beating consensus expectations. Earnings per share came in at $3.16, far surpassing the forecast of $0.99.

ElEVIDYS Revenue Decline: Net product revenue dropped significantly to $330.5 million from $611.5 million in Q1 2025. The decline was primarily attributed to an updated label that only includes the ambulatory patient population for treatment, removing the non-ambulatory indication that had been approved.

Product Mix: ELEVIDYS net product revenue: $102.0 million. PMO franchise net product revenue: $228.6 million. This represents a notable shift, with PMO revenues now exceeding ELEVIDYS for the first time in recent quarters.

Collaboration Revenues: The company reported $54 million in collaboration revenues, including $48 million recognized from Roche declining an option to acquire certain external, early-stage DMD development program rights, and $5.8 million in contract manufacturing collaboration revenues.

Operating Performance: GAAP operating income was $358.4 million. The company maintained a strong cash position with cash and investments totaling $748.3 million as of March 31, 2026.

2026 Guidance: The company reiterated full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $1.2 billion to $1.4 billion, with management stating to model toward the low end of the range. This represents a significant decline from 2025 performance due to label restrictions and market conditions.

Market Sentiment: While earnings beat expectations numerically, the market reaction was mixed due to concerns about ELEVIDYS demand and the strategic direction of the company.
